areas_completed: Structural: one venue path + account ownership

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: Where should the extracted account-provisioning + venue-wiring code live?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: REJECTED THE PREMISE (owner). Relocating the helpers into a new module just moves the mess; the real defect is missing behaviour on the domain objects. Superseded by the decisions below — most of the ~510 lines stop existing rather than moving, and no venue_wiring.py module is created.

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: How far do we take the Account -> Portfolio break?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: FULL BREAK. Account takes no portfolio reference. SimulatedCashAccount(initial_cash) / SimulatedMarginAccount(initial_cash); VenueAccount already clean. The two portfolio-dependent reads move into the method signatures: maintenance_margin(positions, ...) / margin_ratio(equity), supplied by PortfolioHandler which is already their only caller (portfolio_handler.py:569/581). Evidence: SimulatedCashAccount stores self.portfolio and never reads it (oracle path); only SimulatedMarginAccount reads it, at simulated.py:831-833 and :873; VenueAccount.__init__ takes no portfolio at all.
options_presented: Full break as its own phase before 11.1

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: Should Portfolio receive an account factory?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: NO (owner challenged this and was right). A factory parameter would leave Portfolio participating in its own wiring. Because the full break makes Account constructible standalone, Portfolio.__init__ receives a BUILT Account. The duplicate leaf-selection at portfolio.py:176-179 is deleted; VenuePlugin.new_account becomes the sole account factory and loses its portfolio_ref parameter.

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: Does the backtest get the venue abstraction too?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: YES (owner). The backtest registers PaperVenuePlugin and goes through the same one venue path as live — chosen deliberately to keep backtest closer to the real live system. The GATE-01 objection raised against this was WRONG and is withdrawn: venues/__init__.py is deliberately inert by the P5 acceptance gate ('imports NO concretion... importing itrader.venues pulls nothing heavy'), venues/registry.py has zero runtime imports, and itrader.venues is not in the inertness gate's _FORBIDDEN list. D-05 survives intact — 'simulated' is still not a registered venue name; 'paper' is, and it wraps the fill engine.
options_presented: backtest holds the venue registry directly

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: What is the backtest's venue name?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: 'paper' (owner). Backtest and live-paper are the same behaviour — a simulated fill engine over computed accounts — so they get one name. The ('csv', default) -> simulated alias in ExecutionHandler is retired. HIGHEST ORACLE RISK ITEM in the phase: the venue string on every backtest portfolio and order changes, and ~6 test sites read exchanges[('simulated', DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID)] directly.
options_presented: register 'csv' as a paper venue (keep the name)

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: Who builds the SimulatedExchange?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: PaperVenuePlugin builds its own, inside build_bundle — symmetric with OkxVenuePlugin building its own OkxExchange (owner proposal). ExecutionHandler stops minting it (execution_handler.py:290) and is NOT handed one either. This deletes the hoist-into-composition step entirely and dissolves the compose <-> venue-assembly cycle at its source: PaperVenuePlugin(execution_handler.exchanges[...]) at :2028 has nothing left to reach for. Consequence: rng joins EngineContext, since the plugin now builds a stochastic component and determinism requires ONE shared seeded Random injected at the wiring seam (EngineContext currently carries only bus/config/environment/feed/store/sql_engine).

Structural: one venue path + account own.question: Both handlers need the same bundle per (venue, account_id). What owns the memoization?
Structural: one venue path + account own.answer: A memoized VenueBundles provider over (registry, connectors, ctx), held by BOTH ExecutionHandler and PortfolioHandler; each asks for the view it needs (exchange / account). Nothing is passed in pre-built and nothing is mutated from outside. Rationale for rejecting 'ExecutionHandler owns the registry' (the owner's first form): two independent build_bundle callers would produce two OkxExchange instances per account, and OkxExchange.connect() is the sole spawn site for _stream_fills/_stream_orders, so the fill streams would double-spawn. Replaces assemble_venues' eager map plus the exchanges[...] = ... registration loop at live_trading_system.py:2101, so it is a swap, not an addition.
options_presented: ExecutionHandler owns it, PortfolioHandler asks it

deferred_ideas: Registering 'simulated' itself as a venue plugin — rejected: a VenuePlugin models an external venue with credentials/connector/remote account truth; the simulated exchange is the engine's own matcher. D-05 stands.

COMP-03 (Phase 12): config_router.py:402 _dry_validate_copy vs live_trading_system.py:1218 _dry_validate_config_ingress are two implementations of one validation contract — already tracked in Phase 12, not folded here.
